title: Solar Panel Installer
emergingNote: South Africa's load shedding crisis has driven an explosive rooftop solar market. Annual installations grew 10x between 2021 and 2024. Government incentives (Section 12B tax allowance for businesses, rebates for households) continue to fuel demand for qualified solar PV installers.
overview: Solar panel installers design, install, commission, and maintain rooftop and ground-mounted solar photovoltaic systems. South Africa's load shedding emergency accelerated the market dramatically — households and businesses installed record solar capacity. SAPVIA (South African Photovoltaic Industry Association) estimates a shortage of tens of thousands of qualified installers nationally.
dayInTheLife: A solar installer arrives at a Cape Town residential property to install a 10kWp hybrid solar system. Morning is spent mounting panel rails on the roof tile surface and installing 25 panels. Afternoon involves running DC string cabling to the garage inverter, mounting a 10kWh lithium battery bank, wiring the hybrid inverter, and commissioning the system. The homeowner is shown the monitoring app. At a commercial solar installation in Johannesburg, a lead installer supervises a team of four on a 200kWp flat roof warehouse system — panel layout, ballasted mounting frames, and AC connections to the building's MCC.

marketGap.reason: Severe national shortage of qualified solar PV installers. SAPVIA PV GreenCard, SANS 10142-1 compliance knowledge, and wireman's licence (or qualified electrician supervisor) are the key requirements. Self-employed installers with strong reputations earn exceptional income.
